title_en: Structure and Methodology of the Wafayat
content_en:
Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman is organized alphabetically by the first name of each subject — a conventional arrangement for Islamic biographical dictionaries (tarajim works) that allows efficient reference use. The work covers approximately 855 individuals who lived between the Prophet's era and Ibn Khallikan's own time, focusing on figures who achieved distinction in Islamic scholarship, literature, governance, or the arts.

Ibn Khallikan's methodological approach is distinctive in several ways that set the Wafayat apart from earlier biographical works. First, he restricts himself almost entirely to individuals whose death date (wafah) he can establish with reasonable certainty, hence the title's focus on deaths. He was concerned with historical precision about dates — a scholarly virtue that was not always present in earlier biographical literature — and he frequently records multiple opinions about birth and death dates when his sources disagree, noting which he considers most reliable.

Second, Ibn Khallikan explicitly excludes the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ and the four Rightly-Guided Caliphs from his scope, noting that these figures have been treated extensively in specialized literature and that his purpose is to cover the full range of distinguished individuals in Islamic civilization. This restriction focuses the work on the post-Companion period while acknowledging that the foundational generation is covered elsewhere.

Third, his entries are often substantial literary essays rather than brief notices. For major figures, Ibn Khallikan writes multi-page accounts that include biographical details, scholarly assessments, literary anecdotes, examples of the subject's poetry or prose, and testimonies from contemporaries. This narrative richness transforms the Wafayat from a reference work into something closer to a history of Islamic intellectual civilization.

His sources are extensive and carefully acknowledged. He draws on earlier biographical dictionaries, chronicles, literary anthologies, and direct transmission from scholars he met personally. When he cannot verify information, he says so. This intellectual honesty — acknowledging uncertainty rather than papering over gaps — gives the work a reliability that later biographers drew on with confidence.

The full work runs to approximately eight volumes in modern critical editions, representing an extraordinary personal scholarly achievement produced over a career of continuous learning and revision.
content_id:
Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman disusun secara alfabetis berdasarkan nama pertama setiap tokoh — susunan konvensional untuk kamus biografi Islam (karya tarajim) yang memungkinkan penggunaan referensi yang efisien. Karya ini mencakup sekitar 855 individu yang hidup antara era Nabi ﷺ hingga masa Ibn Khallikan sendiri, dengan fokus pada tokoh-tokoh yang mencapai prestasi dalam keilmuan Islam, sastra, pemerintahan, atau seni.

Pendekatan metodologis Ibn Khallikan khas dalam beberapa hal yang membedakan Wafayat dari karya-karya biografi sebelumnya. Pertama, beliau hampir sepenuhnya membatasi dirinya pada individu-individu yang tanggal wafatnya (wafah) dapat beliau tetapkan dengan kepastian yang wajar, maka judul karya yang berfokus pada wafat. Beliau peduli dengan ketepatan historis tentang tanggal-tanggal — kebajikan keilmuan yang tidak selalu hadir dalam literatur biografi sebelumnya — dan beliau sering mencatat berbagai pendapat tentang tanggal lahir dan wafat ketika sumber-sumbernya berbeda, sambil mencatat mana yang beliau anggap paling andal.

Kedua, Ibn Khallikan secara eksplisit mengecualikan para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ dan keempat Khalifah Rasyidin dari cakupannya, dengan menyatakan bahwa tokoh-tokoh ini telah dibahas secara luas dalam literatur khusus dan bahwa tujuannya adalah mencakup seluruh spektrum individu-individu terkemuka dalam peradaban Islam. Pembatasan ini memfokuskan karya pada periode pasca-Sahabat sambil mengakui bahwa generasi fondasi telah dicakup di tempat lain.

Ketiga, entri-entrinya sering kali merupakan esai sastra yang substansial daripada sekadar catatan singkat. Untuk tokoh-tokoh utama, Ibn Khallikan menulis akun beberapa halaman yang mencakup detail biografi, penilaian keilmuan, anekdot sastra, contoh puisi atau prosa tokoh tersebut, dan kesaksian dari orang-orang sezamannya.
content_ar: Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman is organized alphabetically by the أولاً name of each subject — a conventional arrangement for Islamic biographical dictionaries (tarajim works) that allows efficient reference use. The work covers approximately 855 individuals who lived between النبي ﷺ's era and Ibn Khallikan's own time, focusing on figures who achieved distinction in Islamic scholarship, literature, governance, or the arts. Ibn Khallikan's methodological approach is distinctive in several ways that set the Wafayat apart from earlier biographical works. أولاً, he restricts himself almost entirely to individuals whose الموت date (wafah) he can establish with reasonable certainty, ومن ثَم the title's focus on deaths. He was concerned with historical precision about dates — a العلمي الفضيلة that was not always present in earlier biographical literature — and he frequently records multiple opinions about birth and الموت dates عندما his sources disagree, noting which he considers most reliable. ثانياً, Ibn Khallikan explicitly excludes the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ and the four Rightly-Guided Caliphs from his scope, noting that these figures have been treated extensively in specialized literature and that his purpose is to cover the full range of distinguished individuals in Islamic civilization. This restriction focuses the work on the post-Companion period بينما acknowledging that the foundational generation is covered elsewhere. ثالثاً, his entries are often substantial literary essays rather than brief notices. For major figures, Ibn Khallikan writes multi-page accounts that include biographical details, العلمي assessments, literary anecdotes, examples of the subject's poetry or prose, and testimonies from contemporaries. This narrative richness transforms the Wafayat from a reference work into something closer to a history of Islamic intellectual civilization. His sources are extensive and carefully acknowledged. He draws on earlier biographical dictionaries, chronicles, literary anthologies, and direct transmission from العلماء he met personally. عندما he cannot verify information, he says لذا. This intellectual honesty — acknowledging uncertainty rather than papering over gaps — gives the work a reliability that later biographers drew on with confidence. The full work runs to approximately eight volumes in modern البالغ الأهمية editions, representing an extraordinary personal العلمي achievement produced over a career of continuous learning and revision.
